page: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for intensive computational workloads. It features 104 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors operating at 2.7 GHz, with 52 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 936 GB of RAM, providing 9.0 GB of memory per core. For acceleration, it integrates four NVIDIA H100 GPUs from the Hopper architecture, delivering a combined 319 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 3216 GB NVMe SSD.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional performance across multiple domains. The server achieves top-tier status in Passmark CPU scores, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside top-tier performance in small-block cached memory bandwidth at 6842.06 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, as are Geekbench multi-core, ray tracing, and image processing metrics. For language model execution, the system delivers an average of 221.63 tokens per second for 135M models, a strong 38.11 tokens per second for 7B models, and an average of 4.61 tokens per second for 70B models.

Given its high-density hardware profile, which bundles massive GPU acceleration, high-capacity NVMe storage, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors, this server represents a highly specialized resource tier. The primary tradeoff lies in its specialized architecture, which lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses and exhibits average single-core CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance relative to its massive multi-core capabilities. This configuration is optimized for large-scale workloads such as LLM inference, machine learning training, database operations, and high-performance computing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-performance computing and machine learning workloads. It features 104 dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, 936 GB of RAM, and a 3216 GB local NVMe SSD. The server is equipped with four NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs providing 319 GB of total VRAM. In benchmarks, it exhibits top-tier performance in Passmark CPU tests, encryption, and small-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core processing and LLM inference capabilities. While single-core performance is average, its massive parallel processing power makes it highly efficient for demanding workloads. It is best suited for large-scale database operations, deep learning, and complex scientific simulations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server with 104 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 936 GB RAM, 3216 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 319 GB VRAM for high-performance computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g features 104 Intel vCPUs, 936 GB RAM, and four NVIDIA H100 GPUs for intensive machine learning and database workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring four high-performance GPUs, dedicated processors, and high-speed local NVMe storage for intensive parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs with 319 GB total VRAM
104 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 936 GB of system memory
3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier Passmark CPU, encryption, and memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for LLM inference, database operations, and machine learning
